issues.body: The new workflow commentary states exact +16.7% and +8.3% growth figures. The reviewer contract prohibits hard-coded performance numbers, and these historical values are not load-bearing to explaining why the missing CI invocation is a bug. Keeping them here creates stale, non-canonical performance evidence in a durable workflow comment.
issues.fix_hint: Replace the exact percentages with qualitative wording such as 'grew well past the checked-in 3% regression tolerance'; make the corresponding change in the duplicated gate_count.rs module documentation.
issues.title: Remove hard-coded gate-growth percentages

summary: The workflow change correctly closes the coverage gap by running the non-ignored gate-count and catalog test binaries after the pinned nargo/bb toolchain is installed. However, the added documentation embeds hard-coded performance/gate-growth percentages, contrary to the repository's review policy; those figures will also become stale independently of the code and snapshots.
